clinical_history: 54-year-old female, ex-smoker (20 pack-years), presenting with mild chronic cough. Rule out pulmonary pathology.

findings.primary: A solid, non-calcified pulmonary nodule measuring 8 mm in its greatest dimension is identified in the right upper lobe (series 3, image 47). The nodule has spiculated margins. No internal cavitation or fat density is observed.
secondary: Mild pleural thickening noted bilaterally, more prominent on the right.
No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy detected.
incidental: Mild degenerative changes involving the thoracic spine (T6–T9), consistent with patient age.
Trace amount of pericardial fluid — likely physiological.

impression: 8 mm spiculated pulmonary nodule in the right upper lobe. Given the morphological characteristics and the patient's smoking history, this nodule warrants further evaluation per Fleischner Society guidelines. Recommend 3-month follow-up CT chest and pulmonology referral.
recommendations: Follow-up CT chest with contrast in 3 months to assess nodule stability.
Pulmonology consultation recommended for specialist evaluation.
Consider PET-CT if nodule demonstrates growth or metabolic activity on follow-up.
Smoking cessation support if not already in progress.
Correlate clinically and discuss findings with treating physician.
